dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: lib/ is the canonical domain layer: db.ts (9 importers), validate.ts (9 importers), search.ts (8 importers), geo.ts (6 importers) — all consumed by app/api/ routes and pages, correct direction
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Top-level package decomposition is domain-aligned: lib/ holds all domain logic and external integrations, app/api/ holds HTTP handlers per domain capability (search, scrape, vote, restaurant-vote, geo
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: lib/ naming: camelCase is the dominant convention (48 camelCase names) with one snake_case outlier: detect_city in lib/geo.ts. All other lib functions use camelCase (parseQuery, searchRestaurants, com
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: lib/ error strategy is mixed: validate.ts and state-machine.ts throw typed errors (ValidationError, InvalidTransitionError); geo.ts, hours.ts, google-places.ts return null; openrouter.ts uses try/catc
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Domain vocabulary is precise and consistent: parseQuery, searchRestaurants, groupRestaurantsByName, computeFingerprint, validateParsedQuery, assertValidTransition, isOpenNow — all predict behavior wit

dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json production dependencies are minimal: `next`, `react`, `react-dom` only — 3 runtime deps for the whole application

dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: The search boundary is clean: `app/api/search/route.ts` calls `parseQuery → searchRestaurants → groupRestaurantsByName`, three collaborators composed in sequence with explicit inputs and outputs

dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: All 7 API routes (geo, restaurant-vote, scrape, search-more, search, trending, vote) have 0 auth checks — every handler is with_auth: 0 per scan evidence

review_issue_summaries.summary: Restaurant grouping algorithm duplicated across lib/search.ts and components/LoadMoreButton.tsx
lib/geo.ts exports two names for the same function; callers use different aliases creating inconsistent cross-module references
Scrape route acts as both HTTP handler and pipeline orchestrator; orchestration logic belongs in lib/
Restaurant status badge map duplicated across three files with inconsistent shapes
lib/geo.ts exports detect_city in snake_case, violating camelCase convention for all other lib functions
lib/reddit.ts has an import statement at line 52 inside the file body, breaking the universal top-of-file import convention
VoteButton.tsx is a dead sibling of RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx with zero importers; the duplicate vote logic creates convention confusion
tryTransitionToVerified is called after the transaction completes in vote routes; a transition failure returns 500 even though the vote was committed
lib/openrouter.ts getRedditRecommendations returns [] on API failure, making no-results and failure indistinguishable to callers
LoadMoreButton.tsx loadMore() has no catch clause; a fetch failure produces no user feedback and leaves hasMore in an inconsistent state
lib/reddit.ts foodQuery() is named as a transformation but is a no-op that returns its input unchanged
Validator functions accept `Record<string, unknown>` instead of their respective typed interfaces, nullifying type safety at the enforcement boundary
LoadMoreButton re-implements the restaurant grouping algorithm (`normalizeWebsite`, `shouldMerge`, `groupByName`) already present in lib/search.ts, with diverging logic
`foodQuery()` in lib/reddit.ts is an identity function (returns its argument unchanged) — a named abstraction with no behavior
`detectCityFromIp` and `detect_city` are exported as aliases for the same function, splitting callers across two names with no substitution purpose
@prisma/client is in devDependencies rather than dependencies, risking runtime breakage when devDeps are pruned during deployment
The scrape POST handler is a 180-LOC monolith mixing orchestration, Google Places enrichment, community picks fetching, and DB upsert in a single function
lib/validate.ts uses a loop for string-field iteration in validateRedditPost but manual null/empty pairs for all other validators — the inconsistency doubles the code volume for no gain
The /api/search-more endpoint returns raw (ungrouped) restaurants, forcing LoadMoreButton to re-implement the server-side grouping algorithm on the client
The background scrape trigger in the search route fires on every request with <3 results, with no rate-limiting or in-flight deduplication guard
lib/hours.ts isOpenNow is a complex pure function (4 format branches, midnight wrap-around) with 4 importers and zero tests
lib/reddit.ts extractRestaurantName (pure function) and the filtering/relevance logic are untested despite being the core extraction heuristic in the scrape pipeline
The regexFallback function in lib/openrouter.ts is the query-parsing safety net when the LLM is unavailable, but it is untested
lib/rules.ts (checkRule01, checkRule03, checkRule05, tryTransitionToVerified) is only tested through mocked API route boundaries — the actual DB query logic is never exercised
Search concept named 'q', 'query', and 'terms' across sibling API routes
lib/geo.ts exports detect_city and detectCityFromIp as two names for the same function, causing split usage
Vote endpoints accept write operations from any anonymous client with no auth check
POST /api/scrape triggers expensive external API calls (Google Places, Reddit, LLM) with no authentication
foodQuery() in lib/reddit.ts is a pass-through function with no logic — pure scaffolding artifact
Six debug console.log calls left in production scrape pipeline
Restaurant grouping algorithm duplicated verbatim between lib/search.ts and LoadMoreButton.tsx
detect_city alias marked 'backward compatibility' but original snake_case export still actively used in sibling routes
lib/ bundles 4 distinct concern domains in a flat directory with no subdirectory grouping
Reddit env vars captured as module-level constants — fragile in test contexts
scrape/route.ts POST handler is a 180-line function with 5 distinct responsibility clusters
LoadMoreButton re-implements groupByName/shouldMerge/normalizeWebsite already in lib/search.ts
VoteButton and RestaurantFeedbackButton share identical cast() logic duplicated verbatim
TrendingSection useEffect mixes geolocation, IP fallback, and localStorage in one 30-line callback
Restaurant detail page fetches all city restaurants to compute grouping for a single restaurant
foodQuery() is a named transformation function that returns its input unchanged
openrouter.ts JSDoc says 'Gemini Flash' but code uses Llama model
detect_city exported as primary name but alias detectCityFromIp documented as backward-compat alias — roles are inverted
initialUpvotes and initialDownvotes typed as number but guarded with ?? 0 in useState
foodQuery() returns its input unchanged — dead transformation logic
restaurant.upvotes and restaurant.downvotes guarded with ?? 0 after notFound() null check
All validators accept Record<string, unknown>, forcing double-cast through unknown at every call site
RestaurantStatus Prisma enum bypassed with raw string literals in component status lookups
LoadMoreButton casts fetch response to RawRestaurant[] without runtime shape validation

dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: lib/ is the clear logic layer (12 files, avg fan-in 4.2) and app/api/ is the HTTP boundary — dependency direction is consistently one-way: routes import lib, lib does not import routes.
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Top-level directory layout (lib/, components/, app/api/, app/search/, app/restaurant/) maps cleanly to domain roles: pure logic, UI primitives, HTTP boundary, and page-level routing. A new engineer ca
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: lib/ is uniformly camelCase (50 camelCase function/variable names, 0 PascalCase for non-types) — consistent convention.
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts both call tryTransitionToVerified() after the transaction commits. If tryTransitionToVerified() throws (e.g., a DB erro
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: OPEN ISSUE INVESTIGATED: lib/reddit.ts foodQuery() — function does not exist in the current codebase. A full review of lib/reddit.ts confirms no function named foodQuery. This issue should be marked r
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: lib/search.ts exports groupRestaurantsByName as a thin re-export of _groupRestaurantsByName from lib/restaurant-grouping.ts with only an `as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[]` cast — the wrapper adds n
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json dependencies: @prisma/client, next, react, react-dom — all four are production runtime dependencies with no redundancy
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: OPEN ISSUE: app/api/scrape/route.ts POST handler has been refactored — it now delegates to fetchWithFallback (~47 LOC) and enrichAndUpsert (~55 LOC), with the POST handler itself reduced to ~80 LOC of
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: /api/search-more returns raw ungrouped restaurants (app/api/search-more/route.ts returns `results` directly from searchRestaurants). LoadMoreButton.tsx must call groupRestaurants
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: lib/hours.ts isOpenNow has zero tests. The function handles 4 format branches (pipe-separated 12-hour, pipe-separated 24-hour, compact range, '24 hours'/'Closed' literals), midni
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: lib/geo.ts exports detectCityFromIp (async) and extractIp (sync) — mixed sync/async in the same module's public API, flagged in holistic_context.api_surface.sync_async_mix
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: POST /api/scrape has INTERNAL_API_TOKEN header check: reads env var, rejects without header (403) when set. The guard is correctly opt-in — safe for dev, gated in production.
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Previously resolved: foodQuery() pass-through function removed; 8 debug console.log calls removed from scrape pipeline.
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: No mixed JS/TS files — every source file in app/, lib/, components/ is TypeScript (.ts/.tsx). No .js files except config (next.config.js, postcss.config.js, tailwind.config.ts — framework-imposed).
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Directory layout: app/ (Next.js pages + route handlers), components/ (React components), lib/ (business logic + utilities), prisma/ (schema + seed), __tests__ (tests). This is framework-idiomatic and
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: lib/db.ts: module-level singleton creation via `globalForPrisma.prisma ?? new PrismaClient(...)` — PrismaClient is instantiated at import time, not deferred. NODE_ENV is read at module scope to config
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: app/api/scrape/route.ts (225 LOC): This is a Next.js API route — it has zero importers because routes are resolved by the framework via file-system routing, not static imports. It is NOT orphaned in a
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: lib/geo.ts `extractIp(headers: Headers): string` — return type is `string` but the function can return the literal 'unknown' when neither x-forwarded-for nor x-real-ip is present. This is a documented
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: lib/hours.ts `isOpenNow`: complex but genuinely necessary branching for hours format parsing (12h, 24h, compact, closed, 24-hours). All branches are distinct and handle real format variations. No dead

review_issue_summaries.summary: IP extraction duplicated between lib/fingerprint.ts and lib/geo.ts; two lib modules own the same concern
lib/search.ts wraps and re-exports lib/restaurant-grouping.ts, obscuring the true boundary for callers
Scrape route acts as both HTTP handler and pipeline orchestrator; orchestration logic belongs in lib/
Restaurant status badge map duplicated across three files with inconsistent shapes
Scrape is triggered both server-side in api/search and client-side in SearchPoller, creating duplicate pipeline invocations
PRICE_ORDER constant defined identically in two sibling files; one should import from the other or a shared constant
tryTransitionToVerified called after transaction commits in both vote routes; transition failure returns 500 on a committed vote
getRedditRecommendations returns [] on API failure, making network errors indistinguishable from genuine no-results
LoadMoreButton loadMore() has no catch on fetch failure; hasMore and offset remain stale after network error
validateRedditPost and validateExtractedRestaurant throw ValidationError but callers silently catch and continue, undermining the validation contract
lib/rules.ts functions named after opaque rule codes (checkRule01, checkRule03, checkRule05) rather than business intent
SearchPoller.tsx is named as a passive poller but actively initiates a scrape POST request
lib/search.ts groupRestaurantsByName re-export uses `as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[]` to paper over a structural type mismatch rather than aligning types
lib/state-machine.ts exports isValidTransition and validNextStates which have no production callers — speculative API surface
lib/validate.ts uses a loop for RedditPost string fields but manual null/empty pairs for all other validators — persistent inconsistency doubles code volume
searchRestaurants and countSearchResults in lib/search.ts duplicate the Prisma where-condition construction (~40 LOC)
app/api/scrape/route.ts calls parseQuery(query) twice for the same input — once inside fetchWithFallback and once in the POST handler at line 194
/api/search-more returns ungrouped restaurants forcing LoadMoreButton to re-implement server-side grouping on the client
Background scrape trigger in /api/search fires on every low-result request with no rate-limiting or in-flight deduplication guard
fetchWithFallback mutates caller-owned state via an advance() callback — a shared-mutable-state pattern across a function boundary
lib/hours.ts isOpenNow is a complex pure function with 4 format branches and midnight wrap-around logic, with zero tests and 4 production importers
lib/reddit.ts core extraction heuristics (extractRestaurantName, isValidRestaurantName, isPostRelevantToQuery, isRecommendationRequest) have zero tests despite being the pipeline's primary logic
lib/openrouter.ts regexFallback is the query-parsing safety net when LLM is unavailable but has zero tests
lib/rules.ts rule implementations are mocked out in all tests — the actual Prisma DB query logic is never exercised
lib/restaurant-grouping.ts groupRestaurantsByName, shouldMerge, and normalizeWebsite have zero tests despite complex merging logic
lib/geo.ts, lib/search.ts, and lib/reddit.ts mix sync and async exports in their public API surfaces
GET /api/search and GET /api/search-more return different response envelope shapes for the same result type
GET /api/geo returns raw client IP and full Nominatim address data in production JSON response
lib/validate.ts repeats identical null/empty check pattern across 9 validator functions without abstraction
STOP_WORDS constant defined independently in lib/search.ts and lib/reddit.ts with overlapping content
lib/ is flat with 12 files spanning three distinct concern groups: external APIs, domain rules, and infrastructure utilities
Reddit API credentials read at module scope, fragile in testing
Mutable OAuth token cache is module-level singleton, leaks across requests in serverless
STATUS badge lookup map defined independently in three files
PRICE_ORDER sort constant duplicated between search page and LoadMoreButton
Query string parsed twice (two LLM calls) within a single scrape request
TrendingSection mixes geolocation strategy, fetch orchestration, and rendering in one component body

app/api/scrape/route.ts (222 LOC, highest signal density) has no direct test coverage
lib/geo.ts, lib/search.ts, and lib/reddit.ts mix sync and async exports in their public API surfaces
GET /api/search and GET /api/search-more return structurally incompatible response envelopes for the same resource type
INTERNAL_API_TOKEN check on /api/scrape is conditional — endpoint is fully open when env var is unset
lib/validate.ts repeats identical null/undefined + empty-string check pairs ~20 times with a loop/manual inconsistency
validateExtractedRestaurant is called in lib/reddit.ts inside silent try-catch blocks that void the validation contract
lib/ is flat with 12 files spanning three distinct concern groups with no directory-level navigation aid
lib/reddit.ts reads OAuth credentials from process.env at module scope, making them stale if env vars are set after import
lib/reddit.ts cachedToken is a module-level mutable singleton that persists across test runs when the module is cached
lib/state-machine.ts defines isValidTransition and validNextStates as private functions with no callers
components/RestaurantCard.tsx constructs the same Google Maps URL template string twice within the file
TrendingSection.tsx geolocation branching has high cyclomatic complexity but it is inherent to the async feature
app/api/scrape/route.ts is a live Next.js HTTP entry point, not a dead orphan — static analysis misclassifies it
High cyclomatic complexity in 9 files is driven by independent input-validation branching, not a shared design pattern requiring systemic fix
lib/validate.ts interfaces declare all fields as optional but validators throw if fields are absent — TypeScript type is weaker than the runtime contract
Vote routes call validateVoteInput after already enforcing direction values — validation is redundant after explicit guard
fetchWithFallback in app/api/scrape/route.ts receives a 'state' parameter that is never read and returns a stale value
lib/search.ts countSearchResults is declared async but never uses await — the async keyword is redundant
lib/validate.ts validator input interfaces declare required fields as optional, undermining TypeScript enforcement on pre-write guards
RestaurantWithRecommendations.status typed as RestaurantStatus enum but GroupableRestaurant.status typed as string — double cast required at every boundary
